sanskrit:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः।
रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते॥
transliteration:
viṣayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ |
rasa-varjaṃ raso ’py asya paraṃ dṛṣṭvā nivartate ||
literal_meaning: Sense-objects turn away from the embodied being who abstains; the taste lingers, yet even this taste ceases after the higher is seen.
contextual_meaning: Forced self-denial only suppresses behavior; genuine disinterest arises when a more fulfilling inner experience is tasted.
psychological_state: Behavioral suppression without cognitive re-evaluation maintains implicit desire; transformative shift occurs when a superior intrinsic reward is sampled.
trigger_scenario: A person deletes social-media apps to curb doom-scrolling but still fantasizes about feeds; after discovering the absorption of creative flow, the urge for endless scrolling naturally fades.
anchor_text: Taste fades when you glimpse the higher joy within.
